exercises.title: Pattern recognition — choose the right tool
exercises.instructions:
For each problem, state which pattern to use and why:
A: Find maximum sum of any contiguous subarray
B: Find if any 3 numbers sum to 0 in unsorted array
C: Count subarrays whose sum equals k (array has negatives)
D: Sort array with only values 0,1,2
E: Answer 10⁶ range sum queries on static array
exercises.expected_solution:
A: Max contiguous subarray sum → KADANE'S ALGORITHM
   O(n) time O(1) space
   curr = max(num, curr+num) — restart when prev sum negative
   Why not SW: SW works only if all positive. Negatives → Kadane.

B: 3 numbers summing to 0, unsorted → SORT + TWO POINTERS
   O(n log n) sort + O(n²) two-pointer = O(n²) total
   Fix one element, TP on rest. Skip duplicates at all levels.

C: Count subarrays sum==k with negatives → PREFIX SUM + HASHMAP
   O(n) time O(n) space
   SW FAILS with negatives. Prefix: map[sum-k] gives count.

D: Sort only 0,1,2 → DUTCH NATIONAL FLAG
   O(n) time O(1) space — one pass, three pointers
   No need to sort in O(n log n). DNF exploits known value range.

E: 10⁶ range sum queries → PREFIX SUMS
   O(n) precompute, O(1) per query
   10⁶ queries × O(1) = O(10⁶). Without PS: 10⁶ × O(n) = unusable.

test_cases.expected:
A: Max contiguous subarray sum → KADANE'S ALGORITHM
   O(n) time O(1) space
   curr = max(num, curr+num) — restart when prev sum negative
   Why not SW: SW works only if all positive. Negatives → Kadane.

B: 3 numbers summing to 0, unsorted → SORT + TWO POINTERS
   O(n log n) sort + O(n²) two-pointer = O(n²) total
   Fix one element, TP on rest. Skip duplicates at all levels.

C: Count subarrays sum==k with negatives → PREFIX SUM + HASHMAP
   O(n) time O(n) space
   SW FAILS with negatives. Prefix: map[sum-k] gives count.

D: Sort only 0,1,2 → DUTCH NATIONAL FLAG
   O(n) time O(1) space — one pass, three pointers
   No need to sort in O(n log n). DNF exploits known value range.

E: 10⁶ range sum queries → PREFIX SUMS
   O(n) precompute, O(1) per query
   10⁶ queries × O(1) = O(10⁶). Without PS: 10⁶ × O(n) = unusable.

hints: Contiguous max sum → Kadane (not sliding window with negatives)
k-sum → sort + two pointers
Subarray count with negatives → prefix sum always

exercises.title: Trace Kadane's algorithm
exercises.instructions:
Trace Kadane's on nums=[-2,1,-3,4,-1,2,1,-5,4].
Show curr and maxSum after each element.
exercises.expected_solution:
nums = [-2, 1, -3, 4, -1, 2, 1, -5, 4]
Start: curr=-2, maxSum=-2

idx=1: num=1,  curr=max(1, -2+1)=max(1,-1)=1,    maxSum=1
idx=2: num=-3, curr=max(-3, 1-3)=max(-3,-2)=-2,  maxSum=1
idx=3: num=4,  curr=max(4, -2+4)=max(4,2)=4,     maxSum=4
idx=4: num=-1, curr=max(-1, 4-1)=max(-1,3)=3,    maxSum=4
idx=5: num=2,  curr=max(2, 3+2)=max(2,5)=5,      maxSum=5
idx=6: num=1,  curr=max(1, 5+1)=max(1,6)=6,      maxSum=6
idx=7: num=-5, curr=max(-5, 6-5)=max(-5,1)=1,    maxSum=6
idx=8: num=4,  curr=max(4, 1+4)=max(4,5)=5,      maxSum=6

Answer: 6 (subarray [4,-1,2,1])

Key: at idx=2, curr restarts awareness (curr=-2 < 0 relative decision)
Actually curr=-2 but we don't restart since max(-3,-2)=-2 (extending better than restarting at -3)

test_cases.expected:
nums = [-2, 1, -3, 4, -1, 2, 1, -5, 4]
Start: curr=-2, maxSum=-2

idx=1: num=1,  curr=max(1, -2+1)=max(1,-1)=1,    maxSum=1
idx=2: num=-3, curr=max(-3, 1-3)=max(-3,-2)=-2,  maxSum=1
idx=3: num=4,  curr=max(4, -2+4)=max(4,2)=4,     maxSum=4
idx=4: num=-1, curr=max(-1, 4-1)=max(-1,3)=3,    maxSum=4
idx=5: num=2,  curr=max(2, 3+2)=max(2,5)=5,      maxSum=5
idx=6: num=1,  curr=max(1, 5+1)=max(1,6)=6,      maxSum=6
idx=7: num=-5, curr=max(-5, 6-5)=max(-5,1)=1,    maxSum=6
idx=8: num=4,  curr=max(4, 1+4)=max(4,5)=5,      maxSum=6

Answer: 6 (subarray [4,-1,2,1])

Key: at idx=2, curr restarts awareness (curr=-2 < 0 relative decision)
Actually curr=-2 but we don't restart since max(-3,-2)=-2 (extending better than restarting at -3)

hints: curr = max(num, curr+num): restart if starting fresh is better
maxSum tracks the best curr seen so far
Answer: 6 from subarray [4,-1,2,1]

exercises.title: Maximum Subarray — Kadane's Algorithm (LeetCode #53)
exercises.instructions:
Find maximum sum contiguous subarray.
nums=[-2,1,-3,4,-1,2,1,-5,4] → 6
nums=[1] → 1
nums=[-1,-2,-3] → -1 (must pick at least one element)

O(n) time, O(1) space.
hints: Start: curr=nums[0], maxSum=nums[0]
curr = max(num, curr+num): restart when extending would be worse
Works for all-negative arrays: returns the least-negative element

exercises.title: Majority Element (LeetCode #169) — Boyer-Moore Voting
exercises.instructions:
Find element appearing more than n/2 times. Guaranteed to exist.
nums=[3,2,3] → 3
nums=[2,2,1,1,1,2,2] → 2

Boyer-Moore voting: O(n) time, O(1) space.
hints: candidate: current leader. count: lead margin
Different element: count-- (cancellation). If count==0: reset candidate
Majority > n/2 → survives all cancellations

exercises.title: Next Permutation (LeetCode #31)
exercises.instructions:
Find the next lexicographically greater permutation, in-place.
nums=[1,2,3] → [1,3,2]
nums=[3,2,1] → [1,2,3] (wrap to smallest)
nums=[1,1,5] → [1,5,1]

Algorithm: (1) find rightmost ascending pair, (2) swap with next-larger element from right, (3) reverse suffix.
hints: Step 1: find rightmost i where nums[i] < nums[i+1]
Step 2: swap nums[i] with rightmost nums[j] > nums[i]
Step 3: reverse nums[i+1..n-1] to get smallest suffix

exercises.title: Longest Consecutive Sequence (LeetCode #128)
exercises.instructions:
Find length of longest consecutive elements sequence. O(n) time.

nums=[100,4,200,1,3,2] → 4 ([1,2,3,4])
nums=[0,3,7,2,5,8,4,6,0,1] → 9

HashSet approach: only start counting from sequence beginnings (where n-1 not in set).
hints: Only count from sequence start: where n-1 is NOT in set
Each element processed at most twice → amortized O(n)
HashSet deduplicates automatically

exercises.title: Rotate Array (LeetCode #189) — 3 reversals
exercises.instructions:
Rotate array right by k steps. In-place O(n) O(1).
nums=[1,2,3,4,5,6,7], k=3 → [5,6,7,1,2,3,4]
nums=[-1,-100,3,99], k=2 → [3,99,-1,-100]

3-reversal trick: reverse all, then reverse [0..k-1], then reverse [k..n-1].
hints: k %= n: handle rotations larger than array length
Order: reverse ALL, then reverse [0..k-1], then reverse [k..n-1]
Reverse is in-place O(1) extra space

exercises.title: Jump Game (LeetCode #55) — greedy O(n)
exercises.instructions:
Given array where nums[i] = max jump from i, can you reach the last index?
nums=[2,3,1,1,4] → true
nums=[3,2,1,0,4] → false (stuck at index 3)

Greedy: track max reachable index. O(n) O(1).
hints: maxReach: farthest index reachable from any index 0..i
If i > maxReach: we're stuck, can't proceed
maxReach = max(maxReach, i + nums[i])

exercises.title: Maximum Product Subarray (LeetCode #152)
exercises.instructions:
Find contiguous subarray with maximum product.
nums=[2,3,-2,4] → 6 ([2,3])
nums=[-2,0,-1] → 0
nums=[-2,3,-4] → 24

Kadane's extension: track both max and min (negatives can flip sign).
hints: Track both maxProd and minProd — negatives flip which is max
Negative element: swap maxProd and minProd before updating
Result: max of all maxProd values seen

exercises.title: Sliding window vs prefix sum — which fails and why
exercises.instructions:
For each problem + approach, state CORRECT or FAILS and why:

A: nums=[-2,1,-3,4,-1,2,1], target=5. Sliding window for 'subarray sum==5'
B: nums=[1,2,1,3], target=5. Sliding window for 'subarray sum==5'
C: nums=[-2,1,-3,4,-1,2,1], target=5. Prefix sum + HashMap for 'subarray sum==5'
D: nums=[1,2,3,4], k=2. Fixed sliding window for max sum of k consecutive
exercises.expected_solution:
A: [-2,1,-3,4,-1,2,1], SW for sum==5 → FAILS
   Array has negatives. Adding -3 or -1 makes window sum go DOWN while window grows.
   SW assumes: shrinking window decreases sum, growing increases sum.
   With negatives: growing might decrease sum → monotonic property broken.
   Wrong answer: SW might miss [4,-1,2,1]=6≠5 or [-2,1,-3,4,-1,2,1]=2≠5
   Correct answer: prefix map finds [4,-1,2]=5 (indices 3-5).

B: [1,2,1,3], target=5. SW → CORRECT
   All positives: growing window always increases sum, shrinking always decreases.
   Monotonic property holds. SW can find [2,1,3]=6? No → [1,2,1]=4? No → expands
   Actually [2,3]=5 found correctly. SW WORKS on all-positive arrays.

C: [-2,1,-3,4,-1,2,1], prefix+HashMap for sum==5 → CORRECT
   Prefix sums work regardless of sign. O(n) time.
   Finds subarrays where prefix[j]-prefix[i]=5.
   Works on any array.

D: [1,2,3,4], k=2. Fixed SW for max k-consecutive → CORRECT
   Fixed window: add right, subtract left. No sign issues for MAX problem.
   Fixed SW always correct for exactly-k-consecutive problems (sum, average, max).

test_cases.expected:
A: [-2,1,-3,4,-1,2,1], SW for sum==5 → FAILS
   Array has negatives. Adding -3 or -1 makes window sum go DOWN while window grows.
   SW assumes: shrinking window decreases sum, growing increases sum.
   With negatives: growing might decrease sum → monotonic property broken.
   Wrong answer: SW might miss [4,-1,2,1]=6≠5 or [-2,1,-3,4,-1,2,1]=2≠5
   Correct answer: prefix map finds [4,-1,2]=5 (indices 3-5).

B: [1,2,1,3], target=5. SW → CORRECT
   All positives: growing window always increases sum, shrinking always decreases.
   Monotonic property holds. SW can find [2,1,3]=6? No → [1,2,1]=4? No → expands
   Actually [2,3]=5 found correctly. SW WORKS on all-positive arrays.

C: [-2,1,-3,4,-1,2,1], prefix+HashMap for sum==5 → CORRECT
   Prefix sums work regardless of sign. O(n) time.
   Finds subarrays where prefix[j]-prefix[i]=5.
   Works on any array.

D: [1,2,3,4], k=2. Fixed SW for max k-consecutive → CORRECT
   Fixed window: add right, subtract left. No sign issues for MAX problem.
   Fixed SW always correct for exactly-k-consecutive problems (sum, average, max).

hints: SW fails with negatives for min/count problems — not for fixed window max
Prefix sum: always works regardless of sign
Rule: if problem mentions negatives + sum equality → use prefix sum

exercises.title: Maximum Sum Circular Subarray (LeetCode #918)
exercises.instructions:
Find max subarray sum in circular array (can wrap around).
nums=[1,-2,3,-2] → 3
nums=[5,-3,5] → 10 (wraps: 5+5)
nums=[-3,-2,-3] → -2 (no wrap)

Key insight: max circular = max(Kadane normal, totalSum - Kadane(min subarray))
hints: Circular max: wrap = total - (minimum subarray NOT included)
Run Kadane for both max and min simultaneously
All negative: return maxSum (can't use circular — would be empty subarray)

exercises.title: Jump Game II (LeetCode #45) — minimum jumps
exercises.instructions:
Find minimum number of jumps to reach last index. Guaranteed reachable.
nums=[2,3,1,1,4] → 2
nums=[2,3,0,1,4] → 2

Greedy BFS-style: O(n) O(1).
hints: currentEnd: end of current jump's reach
When i reaches currentEnd: jump++ (must jump), extend currentEnd to farthest
Loop to n-2: last element doesn't need a jump FROM it

exercises.title: Maximum Gap (LeetCode #164)
exercises.instructions:
Find maximum gap between successive elements in sorted form. O(n) time.

nums=[3,6,9,1] → 3 (sorted: 1,3,6,9, gaps: 2,3,3 → max=3)
nums=[10] → 0

Naive: sort O(n log n). Optimal: Bucket/Radix sort O(n).
hints: Key: max gap must span a bucket boundary (pigeonhole)
Bucket size = (max-min)/(n-1): ensures max gap > bucket size
MaxGap = max gap between consecutive non-empty buckets

exercises.title: Subarray with Given XOR (two-pointer + prefix XOR)
exercises.instructions:
Count subarrays with XOR equal to k.
nums=[4,2,2,6,4], k=6 → 4

Same pattern as subarray sum == k but with XOR instead of sum.
prefix XOR: if xor[j] ^ xor[i] == k, then xor[i] == xor[j] ^ k
hints: XOR prefix: xor ^= n (running XOR)
Need xor[i] = xor[j] ^ k → check map for (xor ^ k)
XOR is self-inverse: a^b=k → b=a^k

exercises.title: Complete M30 T5 Array Synthesis — Full Pattern Toolkit
exercises.instructions:
Build the complete synthesis toolkit:
1. maxSubarray(nums): Kadane O(n) O(1)
2. majorityElement(nums): Boyer-Moore O(n) O(1)
3. nextPermutation(nums): 3-step O(n) O(1)
4. longestConsecutive(nums): HashSet O(n)
5. rotateArray(nums, k): 3 reversals O(n) O(1)
6. All 5 tests pass
hints: 5 independent algorithms — implement each correctly
Kadane: curr=max(num,curr+num)
Boyer-Moore: cand/count voting
